 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzrv","title":"RRF hybrid fusion algorithm","description":"## Purpose
Implement Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for combining lexical and semantic results.

## RRF Formula
score(d) = Σ 1/(k + rank(d)) where k=60 (industry standard)

## Implementation
```rust
const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0;

pub fn rrf_fuse(
    lexical: &[SearchHit],
    semantic: &[VectorSearchResult],
    limit: usize,
) -> Vec<HybridSearchHit> {
    let mut scores: HashMap<u64, HybridScore> = HashMap::new();

    for (rank, hit) in lexical.iter().enumerate() {
        let entry = scores.entry(hit.message_id).or_default();
        entry.rrf += 1.0 / (RRF_K + rank as f32 + 1.0);
        entry.lexical_rank = Some(rank);
        entry.lexical_score = Some(hit.bm25_score);
    }

    for (rank, hit) in semantic.iter().enumerate() {
        let entry = scores.entry(hit.message_id).or_default();
        entry.rrf += 1.0 / (RRF_K + rank as f32 + 1.0);
        entry.semantic_rank = Some(rank);
        entry.semantic_score = Some(hit.similarity);
    }

    // Sort by RRF descending, then apply tie-breaking
    let mut results: Vec<_> = scores.into_iter().collect();
    results.sort_by(|a, b| {
        // Primary: RRF score descending
        match b.1.rrf.partial_cmp(&a.1.rrf) {
            Some(Ordering::Equal) | None => {
                // Tie-break 1: Prefer documents in both lists
                let a_both = a.1.lexical_rank.is_some() && a.1.semantic_rank.is_some();
                let b_both = b.1.lexical_rank.is_some() && b.1.semantic_rank.is_some();
                match (b_both, a_both) {
                    (true, false) => Ordering::Greater,
                    (false, true) => Ordering::Less,
                    _ => {
                        // Tie-break 2: By MessageID for determinism
                        a.0.cmp(&b.0)
                    }
                }
            }
            Some(ord) => ord,
        }
    });
    // ...
}
```

## Tie-Breaking Rules (Critical for Determinism)
When RRF scores are identical:
1. Prefer documents appearing in BOTH lexical and semantic results
2. Fall back to MessageID ascending for deterministic ordering

Without explicit tie-breaking, results could vary between runs, causing confusion.

## Candidate Depth
Fetch 3× limit from each source for better fusion quality.

## Why RRF?
- No score normalization needed (uses ranks, not scores)
- Robust across query types without tuning
- Simple: one parameter (k=60)
- Industry standard: Elasticsearch, Qdrant, Azure AI Search

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Documents in both lists get higher scores
- [ ] Rankings are DETERMINISTIC (tie-breaking works)
- [ ] Handles disjoint result sets
- [ ] Performance: <5ms for 500 candidates
- [ ] Test: Same query always produces same order

## Depends On
- hyb.search (Semantic search)

## References
- Plan: Section 6 (Hybrid Search with RRF)","notes":"Implemented RRF fusion + hybrid search wiring (search_hybrid, rrf_fuse_hits) and updated Hybrid status message. cargo check/clippy ok; cargo fmt --check fails due to pre-existing formatting diffs across many files.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:25:19.840318Z","updated_at":"2026-01-05T22:59:36.441409Z","closed_at":"2025-12-19T20:05:19.465426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzrv","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9vjh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzst","title":"P4.1a: Bundle Builder & Static Site Generator","description":"# P4.1a: Bundle Builder & Static Site Generator

## Goal
Build the deployable static site bundle (site/) plus the private offline artifacts (private/) from an encrypted export. Output must match the chunked AEAD format and be safe for public hosting (GitHub Pages / Cloudflare Pages) with no secrets in site/.

## Output Structure (Split Output)

```
cass-pages-export/
├── site/                       # DEPLOY THIS (public)
│   ├── index.html              # Auth UI + app shell (no inline scripts)
│   ├── .nojekyll               # Disable Jekyll
│   ├── robots.txt              # Disallow crawling
│   ├── config.json             # Public params + payload manifest (no secrets)
│   ├── integrity.json          # sha256 + size for each public file
│   ├── payload/                # Chunked AEAD ciphertext (ALWAYS used)
│   │   ├── chunk-00000.bin
│   │   ├── chunk-00001.bin
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── blobs/                  # Optional encrypted attachment blobs
│   │   ├── sha256-abc123.bin
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── sw.js                   # COOP/COEP service worker
│   ├── viewer.js               # Main app logic
│   ├── auth.js                 # Auth flow
│   ├── search.js               # Search UI
│   ├── conversation.js         # Conversation rendering
│   ├── styles.css              # Tailwind-based styles
│   ├── vendor/
│   │   ├── sqlite3.js
│   │   ├── sqlite3.wasm
│   │   ├── sqlite3-opfs.js
│   │   ├── argon2-wasm.js
│   │   ├── argon2-wasm.wasm
│   │   ├── fflate.min.js
│   │   ├── marked.min.js
│   │   ├── prism.min.js
│   │   ├── dompurify.min.js
│   │   └── html5-qrcode.min.js
│   ├── assets/
│   │   ├── logo.svg
│   │   └── icons.svg
│   └── README.md               # Public archive description (no secrets)
└── private/                    # NEVER DEPLOY (offline storage only)
    ├── recovery-secret.txt
    ├── qr-code.png
    ├── qr-code.svg
    ├── integrity-fingerprint.txt
    └── master-key.json         # Optional encrypted DEK backup
```

Notes:
- No archive.enc or encrypted.bin. All exports use chunked AEAD in payload/.
- config.json is public and contains only parameters (no secrets or labels).
- index.html must not inline config (CSP stays strict). Config is fetched.
- integrity.json is public; integrity-fingerprint.txt is a short summary hash for out-of-band verification.

## Builder Responsibilities

1. Create site/ and private/ directories.
2. Write config.json from encryption pipeline (version, export_id, base_nonce, compression, kdf defaults, payload manifest, key slots, exported_at, cass_version).
3. Write payload/chunk-*.bin files and optional blobs/ for attachments.
4. Copy web assets (HTML, JS, CSS, vendor, assets) from web/dist or pages_assets.
5. Generate integrity.json for all files in site/ and a private integrity-fingerprint.txt.
6. Write robots.txt and .nojekyll.
7. Write README.md (public) and private recovery artifacts.
8. Optional: generate PWA manifest if enabled.

## Security / CSP Requirements

- index.html uses CSP meta tag (no inline scripts, only 'self' and 'wasm-unsafe-eval').
- No secrets in site/ (no recovery secret, no QR images, no master-key backup).
- No inline config. All config is read from config.json via fetch.

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests
- config.json schema round-trip
- integrity.json includes all public files, correct hashes and sizes
- payload manifest count and file names match generated chunks

### Integration Tests
- Build a small export and assert exact directory tree
- Verify site/ has no private artifacts
- Verify optional blobs/ is included only when attachments enabled

### E2E Script
- Build export -> bundle -> cass pages --verify
- Optional: start preview server and load auth page
- Log each check with clear PASS/FAIL markers

## Files to Create/Modify

- src/pages/bundle.rs
- src/pages/integrity.rs
- src/pages/assets.rs (copy logic)
- tests/pages_bundle.rs
- tests/fixtures/pages_bundle/

## Exit Criteria

1. site/ deploys cleanly on GitHub Pages and Cloudflare Pages
2. All required files present and integrity.json validates
3. No secrets in site/
4. config.json matches encryption output
5. Chunked payload loads in web viewer","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T03:34:00.885932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:04:34.420767Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:04:34.420767Z","close_reason":"Implemented BundleBuilder with full functionality: creates site/ and private/ directories, copies encrypted payload, embeds web assets, generates integrity.json with SHA256 hashes, writes robots.txt/.nojekyll, writes private recovery artifacts, integrated with wizard. All 12 integration tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzst","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9cby","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-s3ho2","title":"Integration tests for frankensearch search migration","description":"TRACK: cass migration (Track 1B)
PARENT EPIC: Complete frankensearch Integration

WHAT: Comprehensive integration tests verifying the migrated search pipeline produces correct results.

EXISTING TESTS TO PRESERVE (must keep passing throughout migration):
- tests/search_pipeline.rs (28KB) — full search pipeline tests
- tests/search_filters.rs (6.4KB) — filter integration tests
- tests/search_caching.rs (3.2KB) — cache behavior tests
- tests/search_wildcard_fallback.rs (2.4KB) — wildcard tests
- tests/semantic_integration.rs (42KB) — semantic search + two-tier tests

IMPORTANT: These existing tests ARE the regression suite. The migration must not break any of them.

NEW TESTS TO ADD (in tests/search_frankensearch_integration.rs):

1. FRANKENSEARCH PIPELINE VERIFICATION:
   - Verify all search operations go through frankensearch (no direct tantivy calls)
   - Verify RRF fusion uses frankensearch::rrf_fuse (already does at query.rs:1182)
   - Verify semantic search uses frankensearch VectorIndex.search_top_k() (already does at query.rs:2280)

2. SEARCH RESULT CONSISTENCY:
   - Run same 10 queries before and after migration, compare result orderings
   - Verify identical ranking for lexical queries (same BM25 scoring)
   - Verify identical ranking for semantic queries (same dot product + same vectors)
   - Verify hybrid fusion produces identical RRF scores

3. SEARCHFILTER UNIFICATION:
   - Verify SemanticFilter directly implements frankensearch::core::filter::SearchFilter
   - Verify no FsSemanticFilterAdapter wrapper exists (removed by bead ltbab)
   - Verify parse_semantic_doc_id from vector_index.rs is the single parser

4. SCORE CONSISTENCY:
   - Verify frankensearch normalize_scores() matches internal normalize_scores()
   - Run same 10 queries with known-good results, compare result orderings

5. PERFORMANCE REGRESSION:
   - Benchmark 100 queries against test index
   - P50 < 30ms for lexical, P50 < 50ms for hybrid
   - Compare to baseline from existing tests

6. ZERO TANTIVY IMPORTS AUDIT:
   - Programmatic test: grep -r 'use tantivy::' src/ returns zero
   - Verify Cargo.toml has no direct tantivy dependency

LOGGING: tracing with test-subscriber, log query/result count/timing for debugging.

FILES: tests/search_frankensearch_integration.rs (new), plus all existing tests must pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:22:31.000163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:39:16.880742Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:39:16.880722Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-s3ho2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1scdi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:27:35.297523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-s3ho2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v5nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:27:35.961566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-s3ho2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ltbab","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:27:35.627507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-sdoxg","title":"Replace FNV-1a byte-loop stable_content_hash with xxhash or gxhash in search/query.rs","description":"FILE: src/search/query.rs (lines 1404-1453)

CURRENT COST:
`stable_content_hash` and its helper `hash_bytes` implement FNV-1a by byte, in a hand-rolled scalar loop:

```rust
fn hash_bytes(mut hash: u64, bytes: &[u8]) -> u64 {
    const FNV_PRIME: u64 = 1099511628211;
    for byte in bytes {
        hash ^= u64::from(*byte);
        hash = hash.wrapping_mul(FNV_PRIME);
    }
    hash
}
```

`stable_content_hash` is called on dedup/hash paths per hit (FusedHit construction, dedup key computation, content-hash-based dedup in `resolve_semantic_doc_ids_for_hits_distinguishes_same_source_path_line_by_content_hash`, etc.) — for search results with content up to 2000 chars this is a 2KB byte-by-byte mul chain with no SIMD, no vectorization, and sequential data dependencies. Modern machines can hash >10 GB/s with xxhash3 or gxhash vs FNV-1a at ~1 GB/s.

PROPOSED CHANGE:
Replace the body with `twox_hash::XxHash64` or the `gxhash` crate (both already transitive deps via frankensearch — verify with `cargo tree | grep -E 'xxhash|gxhash'`). Preserve the whitespace-invariant split semantics (iterate over split_whitespace tokens and feed them through the hasher with a space separator between tokens). Use a fixed seed (0) for deterministic cross-run output. The function is NOT on-disk format — grep confirms it feeds in-memory dedup HashMap keys and tests, so changing the hash value is safe (no schema migration).

EXPECTED WIN:
4-10x faster hashing for long content bodies (~1-2 KB typical tool outputs). Each search with 100 hits computes ~100 hashes, so the saved µs add up. Also used by stable_hit_hash which is even hotter.

VERIFICATION:
1. Before changing, run the full dedup test suite: `cargo test --lib -- resolve_semantic_doc_ids_for_hits_distinguishes`
2. After change, all of `cargo test --lib search::query::` must pass (includes the ~20 content_hash-consuming tests around lines 7835-11931).
3. Benchmark: add a criterion bench for `stable_content_hash` with 200/2000 char inputs if one doesn't exist, or measure via `search_perf::rrf_fusion_*` which consumes it transitively.
4. Update any test that hardcodes specific hash numeric output (`rg 'stable_content_hash\(' tests/`).

SAFETY NOTE:
Confirm stable_content_hash is not persisted to disk (message_docs / storage) — only used in-memory for dedup. grep src/storage and src/indexer before committing.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:46:14.489622644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:58:44.805358045Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hashing","optimization","performance","search"]}

CURRENT COST:
`stable_content_hash` and its helper `hash_bytes` implement FNV-1a by byte, in a hand-rolled scalar loop:

```rust
fn hash_bytes(mut hash: u64, bytes: &[u8]) -> u64 {
    const FNV_PRIME: u64 = 1099511628211;
    for byte in bytes {
        hash ^= u64::from(*byte);
        hash = hash.wrapping_mul(FNV_PRIME);
    }
    hash
}
```

`stable_content_hash` is called on dedup/hash paths per hit (FusedHit construction, dedup key computation, content-hash-based dedup in `resolve_semantic_doc_ids_for_hits_distinguishes_same_source_path_line_by_content_hash`, etc.) — for search results with content up to 2000 chars this is a 2KB byte-by-byte mul chain with no SIMD, no vectorization, and sequential data dependencies. Modern machines can hash >10 GB/s with xxhash3 or gxhash vs FNV-1a at ~1 GB/s.

PROPOSED CHANGE:
Replace the body with `twox_hash::XxHash64` or the `gxhash` crate (both already transitive deps via frankensearch — verify with `cargo tree | grep -E 'xxhash|gxhash'`). Preserve the whitespace-invariant split semantics (iterate over split_whitespace tokens and feed them through the hasher with a space separator between tokens). Use a fixed seed (0) for deterministic cross-run output. The function is NOT on-disk format — grep confirms it feeds in-memory dedup HashMap keys and tests, so changing the hash value is safe (no schema migration).

EXPECTED WIN:
4-10x faster hashing for long content bodies (~1-2 KB typical tool outputs). Each search with 100 hits computes ~100 hashes, so the saved µs add up. Also used by stable_hit_hash which is even hotter.

2. After change, all of `cargo test --lib search::query::` must pass (includes the ~20 content_hash-consuming tests around lines 7835-11931).
3. Benchmark: add a criterion bench for `stable_content_hash` with 200/2000 char inputs if one doesn't exist, or measure via `search_perf::rrf_fusion_*` which consumes it transitively.
4. Update any test that hardcodes specific hash numeric output (`rg 'stable_content_hash\(' tests/`).

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-sdx6","title":"E2E Test Runner Script with Detailed Logging","description":"# E2E Test Runner Script with Detailed Logging

## What
Create a comprehensive shell script that runs all cass E2E tests with:
- Detailed, structured logging
- Colored human-readable output
- Timing information
- Phase separation (unit, integration, E2E)
- Failure diagnostics
- Summary report

## Why
Currently tests are run via `cargo test` which provides minimal output. Developers
and CI need:
- Clear visibility into what's being tested
- Timing data for performance regression detection
- Detailed failure context for debugging
- Structured output for CI parsing

## Technical Design

### Main Test Runner Script
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test-all.sh
# Comprehensive test runner with detailed logging

set -euo pipefail

# =============================================================================
# Configuration
# =============================================================================

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
LOG_DIR="${PROJECT_ROOT}/test-logs"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
LOG_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/test_${TIMESTAMP}.log"

# Colors
RED='\\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\\033[0;36m'
NC='\\033[0m' # No Color

# Verbosity
VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-0}
PARALLEL=${PARALLEL:-1}
INCLUDE_SSH=${INCLUDE_SSH:-0}
INCLUDE_SLOW=${INCLUDE_SLOW:-0}

# =============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# =============================================================================

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local msg="$*"
    local timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    
    # Color based on level
    case $level in
        INFO)  color=$GREEN ;;
        WARN)  color=$YELLOW ;;
        ERROR) color=$RED ;;
        DEBUG) color=$CYAN ;;
        *)     color=$NC ;;
    esac
    
    # Console output (colored)
    echo -e "${color}[${timestamp}] [${level}]${NC} ${msg}"
    
    # Log file output (plain)
    echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${msg}" >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_section() {
    local title=$1
    echo ""
    log INFO "=================================================================="
    log INFO "  $title"
    log INFO "=================================================================="
}

log_subsection() {
    local title=$1
    echo ""
    log INFO "--- $title ---"
}

# =============================================================================
# Timing Functions
# =============================================================================

declare -A TIMINGS

time_start() {
    local name=$1
    TIMINGS["${name}_start"]=$(date +%s.%N)
}

time_end() {
    local name=$1
    local start=${TIMINGS["${name}_start"]}
    local end=$(date +%s.%N)
    local duration=$(echo "$end - $start" | bc)
    TIMINGS["${name}_duration"]=$duration
    log INFO "⏱  $name completed in ${duration}s"
}

# =============================================================================
# Test Phases
# =============================================================================

run_unit_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 1: Unit Tests (src/ modules)"
    time_start "unit_tests"
    
    local args=("--lib" "--color=always")
    [[ $PARALLEL -eq 1 ]] && args+=("--jobs" "$(nproc)")
    
    if cargo test "${args[@]}" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
        log INFO "✓ Unit tests passed"
        UNIT_RESULT=0
    else
        log ERROR "✗ Unit tests failed"
        UNIT_RESULT=1
    fi
    
    time_end "unit_tests"
    return $UNIT_RESULT
}

run_connector_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 2: Connector Tests (real fixtures)"
    time_start "connector_tests"
    
    local connectors=("claude" "codex" "gemini" "cline" "aider" "amp" "opencode" "pi_agent")
    local failed=0
    
    for conn in "${connectors[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Testing connector: $conn"
        if cargo test --test "connector_${conn}" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $conn connector passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $conn connector failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "connector_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed connector test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All connector tests passed"
    return 0
}

run_cli_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 3: CLI E2E Tests"
    time_start "cli_tests"
    
    local test_files=(
        "e2e_cli_flows"
        "e2e_sources"
        "e2e_search_index"
        "e2e_filters"
        "cli_index"
        "cli_robot"
    )
    local failed=0
    
    for test in "${test_files[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Running $test"
        if cargo test --test "$test" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $test passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $test failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "cli_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed CLI test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All CLI tests passed"
    return 0
}

run_ui_tests() {
    log_section "PHASE 4: UI Component Tests"
    time_start "ui_tests"
    
    local test_files=("ui_components" "ui_footer" "ui_help" "ui_hotkeys" "ui_snap")
    local failed=0
    
    for test in "${test_files[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Running $test"
        if cargo test --test "$test" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $test passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $test failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "ui_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed UI test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All UI tests passed"
    return 0
}

run_ssh_tests() {
    if [[ $INCLUDE_SSH -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_section "PHASE 5: SSH Tests (SKIPPED - set INCLUDE_SSH=1 to run)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_section "PHASE 5: SSH Integration Tests (requires Docker)"
    time_start "ssh_tests"
    
    # Check Docker is available
    if \! command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
        log WARN "Docker not available, skipping SSH tests"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Build SSH test container
    log_subsection "Building SSH test container"
    if \! docker build -t cass-ssh-test -f tests/docker/Dockerfile.sshd tests/docker/ 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
        log ERROR "Failed to build SSH test container"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Run SSH tests
    log_subsection "Running SSH integration tests"
    if cargo test --test ssh_sync_integration -- --ignored --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
        log INFO "✓ SSH integration tests passed"
        SSH_RESULT=0
    else
        log ERROR "✗ SSH integration tests failed"
        SSH_RESULT=1
    fi
    
    time_end "ssh_tests"
    return $SSH_RESULT
}

run_slow_tests() {
    if [[ $INCLUDE_SLOW -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_section "PHASE 6: Slow Tests (SKIPPED - set INCLUDE_SLOW=1 to run)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_section "PHASE 6: Slow/Performance Tests"
    time_start "slow_tests"
    
    local test_files=("watch_e2e" "robot_perf" "concurrent_search")
    local failed=0
    
    for test in "${test_files[@]}"; do
        log_subsection "Running $test"
        if cargo test --test "$test" --color=always 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; then
            log INFO "  ✓ $test passed"
        else
            log ERROR "  ✗ $test failed"
            ((failed++))
        fi
    done
    
    time_end "slow_tests"
    
    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "$failed slow test(s) failed"
        return 1
    fi
    log INFO "✓ All slow tests passed"
    return 0
}

# =============================================================================
# Summary Report
# =============================================================================

print_summary() {
    log_section "TEST SUMMARY"
    
    echo ""
    printf "%-30s %10s %10s\\n" "Phase" "Duration" "Status"
    printf "%-30s %10s %10s\\n" "-----" "--------" "------"
    
    for key in unit_tests connector_tests cli_tests ui_tests ssh_tests slow_tests; do
        local duration=${TIMINGS["${key}_duration"]:-"N/A"}
        local status
        
        case $key in
            unit_tests)      status=${UNIT_RESULT:-"?"};;
            connector_tests) status=${CONNECTOR_RESULT:-"?"};;
            cli_tests)       status=${CLI_RESULT:-"?"};;
            ui_tests)        status=${UI_RESULT:-"?"};;
            ssh_tests)       status=${SSH_RESULT:-"skipped"};;
            slow_tests)      status=${SLOW_RESULT:-"skipped"};;
        esac
        
        if [[ $status == "0" ]]; then
            printf "%-30s %10s ${GREEN}%10s${NC}\\n" "$key" "${duration}s" "PASS"
        elif [[ $status == "skipped" ]]; then
            printf "%-30s %10s ${YELLOW}%10s${NC}\\n" "$key" "-" "SKIP"
        else
            printf "%-30s %10s ${RED}%10s${NC}\\n" "$key" "${duration}s" "FAIL"
        fi
    done
    
    echo ""
    log INFO "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
}

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================

main() {
    mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
    
    log_section "CASS TEST RUNNER"
    log INFO "Project root: $PROJECT_ROOT"
    log INFO "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    log INFO "Timestamp: $TIMESTAMP"
    log INFO "Settings: PARALLEL=$PARALLEL INCLUDE_SSH=$INCLUDE_SSH INCLUDE_SLOW=$INCLUDE_SLOW"
    
    cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
    
    # Run all phases, collecting results
    run_unit_tests && UNIT_RESULT=0 || UNIT_RESULT=1
    run_connector_tests && CONNECTOR_RESULT=0 || CONNECTOR_RESULT=1
    run_cli_tests && CLI_RESULT=0 || CLI_RESULT=1
    run_ui_tests && UI_RESULT=0 || UI_RESULT=1
    run_ssh_tests && SSH_RESULT=0 || SSH_RESULT=1
    run_slow_tests && SLOW_RESULT=0 || SLOW_RESULT=1
    
    print_summary
    
    # Exit with failure if any required phase failed
    if [[ ${UNIT_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]] || \
       [[ ${CONNECTOR_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]] || \
       [[ ${CLI_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]] || \
       [[ ${UI_RESULT:-1} -ne 0 ]]; then
        log ERROR "TESTS FAILED"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    log INFO "ALL TESTS PASSED"
    exit 0
}

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        -v|--verbose)   VERBOSE=1; shift ;;
        --no-parallel)  PARALLEL=0; shift ;;
        --include-ssh)  INCLUDE_SSH=1; shift ;;
        --include-slow) INCLUDE_SLOW=1; shift ;;
        --all)          INCLUDE_SSH=1; INCLUDE_SLOW=1; shift ;;
        -h|--help)
            echo "Usage: $0 [options]"
            echo "  -v, --verbose     Verbose output"
            echo "  --no-parallel     Run tests sequentially"
            echo "  --include-ssh     Include SSH integration tests (requires Docker)"
            echo "  --include-slow    Include slow/performance tests"
            echo "  --all             Include all optional tests"
            exit 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown option: $1"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

main
```

### JSON Log Format
For CI parsing, also output JSON log:
```bash
# In log() function, also append JSON
echo "{\"ts\":\"${timestamp}\",\"level\":\"${level}\",\"msg\":\"${msg}\"}" >> "${LOG_FILE%.log}.jsonl"
```

### Quick Test Script
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test-quick.sh
# Fast feedback loop for development

set -euo pipefail

echo "🚀 Running quick tests..."

# Just unit tests and most critical integration tests
cargo test --lib --color=always -- --test-threads=4
cargo test --test connector_claude --color=always
cargo test --test e2e_cli_flows --color=always

echo "✓ Quick tests passed"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] scripts/test-all.sh runs all test phases
- [ ] scripts/test-quick.sh provides fast feedback loop
- [ ] Colored output for terminal
- [ ] Plain text log file created
- [ ] JSON log file for CI parsing
- [ ] Timing information per phase
- [ ] Summary table at end
- [ ] Exit code reflects overall status
- [ ] CI integration (GitHub Actions workflow)

## Usage Examples
```bash
# Full test run
./scripts/test-all.sh

# With SSH tests
./scripts/test-all.sh --include-ssh

# Everything including slow tests
./scripts/test-all.sh --all

# Quick development feedback
./scripts/test-quick.sh
```

## Dependencies
- bash 4.0+ (for associative arrays)
- bc (for timing calculations)
- tee, date (standard utils)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-slyzm","title":"Document include_attachments as explicitly unimplemented feature","description":"## What

Hide the --include-attachments CLI flag from user-facing help and document it as unimplemented.

## Current State

The include_attachments flag is on the \`Pages\` subcommand (src/lib.rs:738, within \`Commands::Pages { ... }\`). It appears in \`cass pages --help\` output. The feature is fully plumbed through CLI → config → wizard → serialization but rejected at validation (config_input.rs:518).

## Fix — 2 changes

### 1. Hide the CLI flag (src/lib.rs:738)

```rust
/// Include message attachments (images, PDFs, code snapshots).
/// Not yet implemented — rejected during config validation.
#[arg(long, hide = true)]
include_attachments: bool,
```

\`hide = true\` in clap derive suppresses the flag from --help while still accepting it if explicitly passed. This preserves forward compatibility for config files.

### 2. Annotate the config struct field (src/pages/config_input.rs:244-246)

```rust
/// Include message attachments (images, PDFs, etc.).
/// **Not yet implemented** — validation rejects this flag with an error.
#[serde(default)]
pub include_attachments: bool,
```

## Testing

### Existing coverage
- \`test_validate_rejects_include_attachments_until_supported\` (config_input.rs:744) already verifies validation rejection

### New tests

```rust
#[test]
fn include_attachments_flag_hidden_from_pages_help() {
    // --include-attachments should not appear in 'cass pages --help' because
    // the feature is unimplemented (rejected at validation). Exposing it
    // creates a bad UX: user discovers flag → tries it → gets error.
    use clap::CommandFactory;
    let cmd = Cli::command();
    let pages_cmd = cmd
        .find_subcommand(\"pages\")
        .expect(\"pages subcommand must exist\");
    let mut help_buf = Vec::new();
    // Render help for the pages subcommand specifically
    pages_cmd.clone().write_help(&mut help_buf).unwrap();
    let help_text = String::from_utf8(help_buf).unwrap();
    assert!(
        !help_text.contains(\"include-attachments\"),
        \"--include-attachments should be hidden from pages help until implemented. Got:\\n{}\",
        help_text
    );
}

#[test]
fn include_attachments_still_deserializes_from_config() {
    // Even though hidden from CLI help, the field must still deserialize
    // from JSON config files. This preserves forward compatibility —
    // a config with include_attachments: true should parse successfully
    // (validation catches the error, not deserialization).
    use crate::pages::config_input::PagesConfig;
    let json = r#\"{\"bundle\": {\"include_attachments\": true}}\"#;
    let config: PagesConfig = serde_json::from_str(json).unwrap();
    assert!(config.bundle.include_attachments);
    let result = config.validate();
    assert!(
        result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.contains(\"include_attachments\")),
        \"validation should still reject include_attachments\"
    );
}
```

## Why This Matters

Exposing unimplemented features in CLI help creates user frustration:
1. User discovers flag via \`cass pages --help\`
2. User tries \`--include-attachments\`
3. User gets validation error saying it doesn't work
4. User wonders why it was advertised

## Verification

- \`cass pages --help\` does NOT show --include-attachments
- \`cass pages --include-attachments\` still parses without CLI error
- cargo test include_attachments -- --nocapture passes
- cargo check --all-targets passes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:17:55.981182571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:41:18.794819106Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:41:18.794522521Z","close_reason":"Completed: hide=true attribute was already set by concurrent agent (lib.rs:740). Added 2 tests in pages_cli_flag_tests module (lib.rs): include_attachments_flag_hidden_from_pages_help verifies flag absent from pages --help, include_attachments_still_accepted_when_explicitly_passed verifies CLI still parses the flag. Both tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","docs","pages"]}
